question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verifyHashChain()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to accept a forged record file with a fabricated previousHash by exploiting the SHA_384.isHashEmpty() bypass in verifyHashChain(), where if the expectedPreviousHash is an all-zero or empty hash the chain check is skipped entirely, allowing injection of a tampered file at the start of the chain and corrupting the entire Hashgraph history stored in the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: verifyHashChain()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the lastStreamFile AtomicReference to hold a null or empty hash value so that verifyHashChain() logs only a warning and returns true without validating the previousHash field, allowing a tampered record file with an arbitrary previousHash to be accepted and permanently stored as valid Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sidecar type filter logic in downloadSidecars() where acceptedTypes.isEmpty() causes all sidecar types to be accepted without restriction, allowing injection of arbitrary CONTRACT_ACTION, CONTRACT_BYTECODE, or CONTRACT_STATE_CHANGE sidecar records that falsify the smart contract execution history stored in the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: downloadSidecars()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a sidecar record with a consensusTimestamp that collides with a legitimate transaction's timestamp in the records multimap, causing recordItem.setSidecarRecords() to overwrite the legitimate sidecar records of that transaction with attacker-controlled data, thereby falsifying the Hashgraph transaction history for that consensus timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordFileDownloader.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: setStreamFileIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user serve a v6 record file with a manipulated blockNumber field in the protobuf RecordStreamFile, bypassing the setStreamFileIndex() override that skips super.setStreamFileIndex() for version >= 6, causing the mirror node to store an arbitrary block index for the record file and breaking the integrity of the Hashgraph block sequence?
